title: Sorting Algorithms, Stability, and Ordering Contracts · Scale Transfer
summary: Order customer records by multiple keys while preserving meaningful prior order. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a sorting laboratory with operation counts with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define total, partial, and key-based orderings including tie and missing-value policy.
Trace insertion and selection sorting invariants and identify useful input regimes.
Implement stable merge sort and account for auxiliary space and copying.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
